{{Infobox character
| name = Mell D-One-Twenty-Five
| aliases = Doctor Mell, Mell
| abilities = {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Temporal Field]], decoy-bots, [[Nova-Bots]]}}
| titles = {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Doctor, [[Doctor Nova]]}}
| affiliations = {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Last Horizon]]}}{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|, [[The Zenith Starship|Zenith Starship]]}}
| professions = {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Technicians|Technician]], [[Engineer]], [[Aether Technician]]}}{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|, [[wizard]]}}{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|, [[Aether Technician|master Aether Technician]]}}
| residence = {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Last Horizon]]}}{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|, [[Advocate prison]]}}
| appears_in = The Last Horizon
| introduced = [[The Engineer (novel)|The Engineer]]
}}

''See also: [[Mell D-One-Twenty-Five Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Mell D-One-Twenty-Five, also known as Doctor Mell or [[Doctor Nova]], is a human [[Aether Technician]] characterized by her specialized android gift. She is a master of her craft, possessing the ability to create advanced technology such as [[Nova-Bots]] and highly realistic decoy-bots. Her expertise lies in [[Aethertech]], which she uses to build, repair, and manipulate complex systems. While she is highly skilled, her personality often fluctuates between cocky confidence and high-strung anxiety, frequently seen working feverishly under pressure or managing multiple monitors at once. Mell's interests are primarily driven by her research and personal projects, including a dedicated focus on destroying the [[Iron Legion]]. She has demonstrated significant technical prowess by studying powerful weaponry like the [[Zenith Cannon]] to develop her own firepower and has worked on various complex tasks ranging from repairing androids to analyzing the effects of the [[Evolution Engine]]. Despite her tendency to prioritize her work over galactic affairs, she remains a vital asset whose synergy with other technologies is highly valued by allies.}}

== The Captain ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Mell D-One-Twenty-Five is a location situated within [[Union space]]. The entity associated with this name maintains a [[workshop]] filled with diverse robotic creations and is typically seen wearing a dusty lab coat. This individual also utilizes a console strapped to her wrist and once received a rejection message from [[The Last Horizon]].}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">5</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Mell is a character who has a [[workshop]] containing various robotic creations.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c10">''The Captain'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Mell is a character who wears a dusty lab coat.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c12">''The Captain'', chapter 12</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Mell has a console strapped to her wrist.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c12" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Mell received a rejection message from [[The Last Horizon]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c15">''The Captain'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Mell is a location within [[Union space]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c38">''The Captain'', chapter 38</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== The Engineer ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell D-One-Twenty-Five, an [[Aether Technician]] also known as [[Doctor Nova]], was cloned in a lab from the DNA of four human technicians. While she previously used a decoy bot to evade capture, she was eventually kidnapped by [[Queen Shyrax]] and held in an [[Advocate prison]] cell. As the creator of [[Starhammer]], she designed him to be her protector and built his [[Temporal Field]]. Now serving as the [[Engineer]] for [[The Last Horizon]], Mell utilizes goggles to monitor [[Nova-Bots]] and operates various ship systems. Throughout her service, she has used a helmet to transfer her consciousness into a robot and has even manifested from blue light at the helm of a shuttle.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">64</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Doctor Mell is at a location that can be reached after recruiting the [[Commander]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c2">''The Engineer'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell uses glasses to display the status of her lab and surrounding [[Nova-Bots]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c6">''The Engineer'', chapter 6</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Doctor Mell is currently in an [[Advocate prison]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c7">''The Engineer'', chapter 7</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell is a person whose actions [[Starhammer]] is contemplating.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c8">''The Engineer'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] wonders if something is part of Mell's plan.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c8" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell is someone [[Starhammer]] would want to meet upon learning of her capture.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c10">''The Engineer'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell is someone the team needs to rescue.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c10" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell was dressed as [[Doctor Nova]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c11">''The Engineer'', chapter 11</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell is a Technician who makes things that break into things.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c11" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell is a target indicated by pathfinding spells.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c12">''The Engineer'', chapter 12</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell piloted a decoy bot before being arrested.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c13">''The Engineer'', chapter 13</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell was being kidnapped.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c14">''The Engineer'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell was flailing her hands and legs in the air.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c14" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell was being held in a prison cell.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c15">''The Engineer'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell designed [[Starhammer]] to protect her.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c15" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell was being held by Shyrax.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c15" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell is the creator of [[Starhammer]] and built him to be her protector.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c16">''The Engineer'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell is an [[Engineer]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c16" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell built [[Starhammer]]’s [[Temporal Field]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c16" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell does not have an armor gift.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c16" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell is considered one of the Advocates' greatest enemies.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c17">''The Engineer'', chapter 17</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell was wearing a white jumpsuit of a prisoner.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c17" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell protests [[Horizon]]'s decision to assemble a crew.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c17" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The narrator places a hand on Mell's shoulder.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c17" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell wears a white lab coat over black clothing.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c18">''The Engineer'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell is an [[Aether Technician]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c18" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell is a member of the crew.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c19">''The Engineer'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell is a character who tests the narrator's trustworthiness.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c19" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell has a lab.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c20">''The Engineer'', chapter 20</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell was grown in a lab.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c20" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell was cloned from the DNA of four human [[Aether]] [[Technicians]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c20" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell created [[Starhammer]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c20" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell sabotaged the inventory system of [[Stellar Synthesis Labs]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c20" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell is working on the ship.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c21">''The Engineer'', chapter 21</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell is a character who was observing a half-finished ship.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c24">''The Engineer'', chapter 24</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell is an ally of the narrator.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c28">''The Engineer'', chapter 28</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell uses goggles and works with [[Nova-Bots]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c30">''The Engineer'', chapter 30</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell is someone [[Starhammer]] is searching for.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c31">''The Engineer'', chapter 31</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell is the [[Engineer]] of [[The Last Horizon]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c32">''The Engineer'', chapter 32</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell does not have a repair gift for ships.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c32" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell was taken by [[Queen Shyrax]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c33">''The Engineer'', chapter 33</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell wears glasses.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c34">''The Engineer'', chapter 34</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Mell D-One-Twenty-Five]] is also known as [[Doctor Nova]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c35">''The Engineer'', chapter 35</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell defends [[Starhammer]]'s character.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c36">''The Engineer'', chapter 36</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell has a Technician's gift.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c37">''The Engineer'', chapter 37</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell knows about [[Starhammer]]'s tactics.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c38">''The Engineer'', chapter 38</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell transfers her consciousness to a robot using a helmet.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c38" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell designed the enemy.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c39">''The Engineer'', chapter 39</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell created the [[Evolution Engine]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c39" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell mentions being kidnapped before.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c41">''The Engineer'', chapter 41</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell is a member of the crew of the [[Zenith]] Starship.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c42">''The Engineer'', chapter 42</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] had interactions with Mell.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c45">''The Engineer'', chapter 45</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell is a person who insisted the [[Evolution Engine]] made [[Starhammer]] oblivious.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c46">''The Engineer'', chapter 46</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell operates various ship systems.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c47">''The Engineer'', chapter 47</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell is mentioned by [[Starhammer]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c47" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell manifested from blue light at the helm of one of [[The Last Horizon]]’s shuttles.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c48">''The Engineer'', chapter 48</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The location of Mell is known to others.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c48" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell was waiting in orbit during the heist.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c49">''The Engineer'', chapter 49</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The narrator told [[Starhammer]] where to find Mell.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c49" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell spent five days gathering the rest of her crew.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c50">''The Engineer'', chapter 50</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell can build decoy-bots.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c50" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell was teleported to a nearby shuttle.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c51">''The Engineer'', chapter 51</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell has proof regarding [[Starhammer]]'s secret.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c52">''The Engineer'', chapter 52</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Mell is grieving for [[Starhammer]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c53">''The Engineer'', chapter 53</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== The Knight ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Known popularly as [[Doctor Nova]], Mell D-One-Twenty-Five is a wizard who wears a white lab coat and text-scrolling glasses. After providing [[Nova-Bots]] to [[the Queen]], she prepared to clear out her hotel room before departing her planet. As a crew member of [[The Last Horizon]], she has served as company for [[Horizon]] and was once considered as a candidate to be brought to the [[Titan Forge]]. During her service, she utilized [[Nova-Bots]], held the [[Zenith Cannon]], and previously engaged in combat against [[Starhammer]]. Although she lacks the ability to recognize magical portents, she manages medical [[Aethertech]] via a console in her room and has even cowered behind two large [[Nova-Bots]] during conflict. Following an impact that knocked her unconscious, she later demanded that Raion explain his fighting methods.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">19</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Mell is a person who wears a white lab coat popularized as [[Doctor Nova]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c4">''The Knight'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Mell brushes her coat off with her hands.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c4" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Mell intends to clear out her hotel room before leaving the planet.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c4" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Mell provided [[Nova-Bots]] to [[the Queen]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c4" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Mell is a wizard.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c8">''The Knight'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Mell uses [[Nova-Bots]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c8" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Mell is a person the narrator considers bringing to the [[Titan Forge]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c10">''The Knight'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Mell can keep [[Horizon]] company.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c14">''The Knight'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Mell is a person who previously fought [[Starhammer]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c16">''The Knight'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Mell cowered behind two larger [[Nova-Bots]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c18">''The Knight'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Mell is a crew member of [[The Last Horizon]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c19">''The Knight'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Mell held the [[Zenith Cannon]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c22">''The Knight'', chapter 22</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Mell is someone Varic intends to check on.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c28">''The Knight'', chapter 28</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Mell cannot recognize magical signs and portents.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c28" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Mell has a medical room with a console next to her bed to control the medical [[Aethertech]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c32">''The Knight'', chapter 32</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Mell is a crew member of [[The Last Horizon]] who wears glasses that scroll with text.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c38">''The Knight'', chapter 38</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Mell called her Nova-Bot pilots.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c39">''The Knight'', chapter 39</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Mell is a character who was knocked unconscious during an impact.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c40">''The Knight'', chapter 40</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Mell shouted at Raion to explain how his fighting works after the fight.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c44">''The Knight'', chapter 44</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== The Pilot ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Mell D-One-Twenty-Five is a human technician and master [[Aether]] [[Engineer]] with an android gift who serves as a crew member aboard [[The Last Horizon]]. While her physical body remains on the ship's bridge, she pilots bots and utilizes indistinguishable decoy-bots, once even tackling [[Doctor Oladyne]] into a wall to serve as a distraction. Throughout her duties, she manages consoles and gemstones, insists on [[vault]] security, and coordinates groups of [[Nova-Bots]] to assist with repairs or to collect samples from [[Omega]]'s body. Mell spends much of her time assembling [[Nova-Bots]], installing data cores, and focusing on personal research projects. Her recent activities included instructing the ship to fabricate a nondescript shuttle and attempting to activate the [[Temporal Field]], though she was subsequently restrained in a cot and strapped to a table beneath a helmet before falling comatose in her quarters.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">32</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Mell D-One-Twenty-Five]] is a human Technician with an android gift.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c1">''The Pilot'', chapter 1</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Mell pilots a bot and has a real body located on the bridge of [[The Last Horizon]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c3">''The Pilot'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Mell tackled [[Doctor Oladyne]] into a wall as a decoy-bot.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c4">''The Pilot'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Mell is on the ship and uses a decoy-bot.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c4" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Mell is breaking into a console stolen from [[Solstice]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c7">''The Pilot'', chapter 7</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Mell is a character who works with consoles and gemstones.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c8">''The Pilot'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Mell is a crew member who insists on security measures for the [[vault]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c8" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Mell flagged a location as suspicious in the Vice Chief's console.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c10">''The Pilot'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Mell asks if the heart of the [[Grand Hive]] was scooped out and made to disappear.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c10" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Mell states that core components of an [[Iron Hive]] are a big pile of raw materials.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c10" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Mell cannot do anything without [[Nova-Bots]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c14">''The Pilot'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Mell uses androids to assist in repairs.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c14" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Mell coordinates [[Nova-Bots]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c14" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Mell noted that a dive lasted forty-eight minutes.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c15">''The Pilot'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Mell is working with thick glasses and assembling a Nova-Bot.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c19">''The Pilot'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Mell uses decoy-bots that are difficult to distinguish from reality by sight.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c20">''The Pilot'', chapter 20</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Mell installs data cores into [[Nova-Bots]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c20" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Mell was chewing on three sugar-sticks.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c24">''The Pilot'', chapter 24</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Mell focuses on her own research and personal projects.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c26">''The Pilot'', chapter 26</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Mell's mind is merely human.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c29">''The Pilot'', chapter 29</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Mell used hand-size [[Nova-Bots]] to take samples of [[Omega]]'s body.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c30">''The Pilot'', chapter 30</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Mell D-One-Twenty-Five]] is an [[Engineer]] and a master [[Aether Technician]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c32">''The Pilot'', chapter 32</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Mell D-One-Twenty-Five]] has an android gift.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c32" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Mell wears a long white coat with the seven-pointed star of [[The Last Horizon]] on its breast.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c32" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Mell is a character who cackled and slapped Sola on the shoulder.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c32" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Mell instructed [[Horizon]] to fabricate a nondescript shuttle.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c42">''The Pilot'', chapter 42</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Mell coordinates groups of [[Nova-Bots]] aboard [[The Last Horizon]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c46">''The Pilot'', chapter 46</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Mell can take control of [[Starhammer]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c46" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Mell attempted to activate the [[Temporal Field]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c46" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Mell was restrained in a cot.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c46" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Mell was strapped to a table beneath a helmet.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c47">''The Pilot'', chapter 47</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Mell was comatose in her own quarters.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c49">''The Pilot'', chapter 49</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Not that kind of Technician. I don’t break into things… Mell pulled out a case and cracked it open. “…I make the ones who do.”<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c11" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|“I should have tried to save him,” she’d said. “I designed him to protect me.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c15" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|“I have the doctor.” Mell’s scream was background noise.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c15" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|“Hey, wait, he’s not that bad. He wouldn’t throw away his own people.”<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c36" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|And the Evolution Engine?”

“It was my attempt to work around that restriction.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c39" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{The Last Horizon nav}}

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:The Captain]]
[[Category:The Engineer]]
[[Category:The Knight]]
[[Category:The Pilot]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon characters]]
[[Category:Titled characters]]
[[Category:Viewpoint characters]]
